title: GPU Parallelization in Attention
concept: Self-attention computes relations for all words in parallel during training, letting models train on massive datasets in a fraction of the time.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of self-attention when it comes to speed?

options.text: It allows all words to be analyzed at the same time.
It limits the model to only processing three-letter words.
It requires the computer to turn off its graphics processor.

exercises.explain: Self-attention calculates how all words in a sentence relate to each other. Because it can do this for all words at the same time, it can run in parallel on GPUs, drastically speeding up training.

cards.title: Reading Everything at Once
cards.body: Before modern AI, computers read text like humans do: one word at a time, from left to right. This made training large models painfully slow. Today's AI models use GPUs to look at entire libraries of text all at once, speeding up learning by thousands of times!

cards.title: What is GPU Parallelization?
cards.body: GPU Parallelization is the ability of graphics cards to perform thousands of mathematical calculations at the exact same time. In self-attention, this means the AI doesn't wait to read word by word; it processes all relationships across the entire sentence simultaneously.

cards.title: The Classroom Questionnaire
cards.body: Imagine a classroom with 100 students. An old RNN model is like a teacher interviewing each student one by one in a long line. An attention model on a GPU is like handing a questionnaire to all 100 students at the exact same time. Everyone writes down their answers simultaneously, finishing the task in seconds instead of hours.

cards.title: Simultaneous Connections
cards.body: When training a modern AI on a sentence like "The dog chased the cat because it was fast," a GPU doesn't calculate the meaning of "dog," then "chased," then "cat" in a sequence. Instead, it fires up thousands of tiny processors to calculate how "it" connects to "dog" and "cat" at the very same instant.

cards.title: Not Just for Gaming
cards.body: Many people think GPUs are only for video games and 3D graphics. While they were invented for games, the math needed to render 3D pixels is the exact same math needed to calculate self-attention connections. This is why gaming hardware powered the modern AI revolution!

cards.title: Visualizing Parallelism
cards.body: Let's compare how old text models process data versus how modern GPU-powered attention models do it.

right.text: Processes all words and their connections at the same time. Fast and efficient.

left.text: Processes 'Word 1', then waits to process 'Word 2', then 'Word 3'. Slow and serial.

exercises.prompt: Why is self-attention highly compatible with GPUs?

options.text: It computes relationships for all words in parallel.
It forces the computer to read words one by one.
It deletes unimportant words to save memory.
It slows down training to ensure accuracy.

exercises.explain: Self-attention computes the relationships between all words simultaneously, which allows the training process to happen much faster on parallel-processing hardware like GPUs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-attention must wait for the first word's calculations to finish before it can start calculating the second word.
exercises.explain: Self-attention is designed to calculate all word connections at the same time, which is why it can take full advantage of GPU parallelization.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how GPUs handle attention calculations.
exercises.sentence: Self-attention allows computers to process all word relationships in [blank] rather than one after another.
exercises.explain: Unlike older models that read step-by-step, self-attention allows the GPU to compute word relationships in parallel, saving massive amounts of time.

exercises.prompt: Identify which statement describes parallel GPU processing instead of sequential processing.

options.text: Human: "I am writing down words one by one in a notebook."
AI Assistant: "I am calculating the relationship values for all 50 words in this paragraph at the exact same millisecond."
Human: "I am reading the first word, then moving to the second word."

exercises.explain: The AI Assistant uses parallel computing, which calculates multiple connections at the same time, unlike the step-by-step sequence of the human.

exercises.prompt: How does a GPU speed up the self-attention process during AI training?

options.text: It processes words one by one in a sequence, only faster.
It calculates all word connections at the exact same time.
It skips calculating connections to save time.
It only reads the first and last words of a sentence.

exercises.explain: GPUs speed up self-attention by calculating how every word relates to every other word at the exact same moment. This massive, simultaneous calculation eliminates the slow, step-by-step processing of older models.
